career.organization: US Postal Service, North Haven, Connecticut
career.notes: Postmaster of N. Haven pre-dentistry per 1907 obit.
Per 1907 obit: 'for seventeen years a dentist in Chapel street.'

cross_connections.display_label: First cousins through their Blakeslee mothers, Sophronia and Lavinia
First cousins through Stiles brothers Isaac Lorenzo and Henry Hobart
Two Stiles cousins, both dead on their birthday

narrative_blocks.header: Seventeen years a Chapel Street dentist, New Haven
narrative_blocks.body: Isaac Wadsworth Stiles practiced dentistry on New Haven's Chapel Street for seventeen years before dying of heart disease at his West Haven home in February 1907 age 64. Before dentistry he had served as postmaster in North Haven, where his father Isaac Lorenzo Stiles ran the family brick manufacturing company.

Isaac Wadsworth Stiles was born February 28, 1843 and died of heart disease on February 28, 1907 — his sixty-fourth birthday. He had been an invalid for several years before his death at home in West Haven.

sources.label: New Haven Morning Journal and Courier obit, Feb 11, 1907

narrative_blocks.header: Body shipped from Tucson to New Haven, 1925
narrative_blocks.body: Mabel Stiles Warner died at her Tucson Arizona home on January 6, 1925 age 46, having moved there only eleven months earlier. Her body was shipped back to New Haven Connecticut by the Reilly Undertaking Company, where she joined her parents at the family plot in New Center Cemetery, North Haven.

narrative_blocks.header: Tucson home eleven months before her death
narrative_blocks.body: She died 6 January 1925 at her South Scott Street home in Tucson Arizona — her residence of only the past eleven months. The move had likely been a health-driven relocation to desert air, common among consumptive patients of the early twentieth century.

narrative_blocks.header: 1899 home wedding with palms and Fichtl's orchestra
narrative_blocks.body: She married Charles Bennet Warner in 1899 at her father's West Haven home at 6 PM — described as a 'pretty home wedding' with rich white satin gown, tulle veil fastened with lilies of the valley, an improvised altar of palms and spring blossoms, and Fichtl's orchestra playing bridal music. Rev R H Gessner of Christ Episcopal Church officiated; the couple took a two-week wedding trip after a wedding supper.

narrative_blocks.header: Sister escorted body two thousand miles home
narrative_blocks.body: Her sister Mrs Edward Stiles of Hartford was with Mabel at her Tucson death and accompanied the body home. The Reilly Undertaking Company shipped Mabel's body back to New Haven by rail — a journey of roughly two thousand miles under solo sisterly escort, for burial at New Center Cemetery.

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name 'Lorenzo' carries his grandfather Isaac Lorenzo Stiles's middle name through three generations (Isaac Lorenzo Stiles → Dr. Isaac Wadsworth Stiles → Stanley Lorenzo Stiles). His uncle Frank Lorenzo Stiles (HD3939) also shares the same middle name.

narrative_blocks.header: Mother died about a year after his birth
narrative_blocks.body: Stanley Lorenzo Stiles was born in 1888 to Dr. Isaac Wadsworth Stiles DDS and his first wife Margaret Ella Dickerman Stiles. His mother died on October 21, 1889 — approximately one year after his birth — and the death may have been childbirth-related, though the cause is not specified in available records. He was raised by his father, who eventually remarried.

narrative_blocks.header: Lorenzo middle name carried through three generations
narrative_blocks.body: Stanley's middle name 'Lorenzo' was a family tradition carried across three generations: his grandfather Isaac Lorenzo Stiles, his uncle Frank Lorenzo Stiles, and Stanley himself all bore it. The repeated-middle-name pattern was characteristic of the late-nineteenth-century Stiles branch's deliberate genealogical naming conventions, preserving a distinctive ancestor's mark forward across the line.

narrative_blocks.header: Dual Hooker descent through both Stiles lines
narrative_blocks.body: Stanley Lorenzo Stiles inherited dual Hooker descent paths from his father Dr. Isaac Wadsworth Stiles — who himself was double-descended through both his father (Isaac Clark Stiles branch) and his mother (Mabel Stiles Frost branch). Both lines converge at Isaac Stiles born 1729 — making Stanley a doubly-rooted Hooker descendant on his paternal side alone, with the non-Hooker maternal side adding no additional paths.

narrative_blocks.header: Lived to about sixty-one, marriage not documented
narrative_blocks.body: Stanley Lorenzo Stiles died in 1949 at about age sixty-one. He was listed as 'Stanley L. Stiles surviving' in his father's February 1907 obituary at about age nineteen. Marriage and any descendants are not documented in available records.

burialCemetery.name: New Center Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Cemetery in North Haven CT. Major Stiles family burial ground spanning multiple generations — Isaac Stiles (HD3922) + Lois Cooper (I00524); infant son Sterling (HD3933); son Isaac Lorenzo (HD3934). Additional Stiles burials to be added in subsequent stages.
